title: Cynanchica rupicola
intro: Cynanchum rupicola, commonly known as the rock vine or mountain milkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asclepiadaceae family. Traditionally, Cynanchum rupicola has been used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits, which may include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, particularly aristolochic acid, which has been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Cynanchum rupicola is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
